---
title: "Rumizi Assistant"
description: "A copilot inside the vendor workspace. It can create categories, add items, change prices, generate images, and more — always with your confirmation."
sidebarTitle: "Overview"
icon: "wand-magic-sparkles"
---

Rumizi Assistant is the AI copilot inside your workspace. Instead of
clicking through menus and dialogs, you can tell it what you want and it
drafts the change for you — then you **Apply** it.

## Where to find it

The assistant appears as a floating button on every workspace page at
`/your-venue/w/*`. It isn't shown on the public customer menu.

On the **Menu editor** pages, you can also open the assistant from an
inline control in the header — same panel, closer to hand.

## What it can do

<Columns cols={2}>
  <Card title="Build menus">
    Create categories, add items, change prices, write descriptions, set
    stock, rename things in bulk.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Schedules and tags">
    Add and remove time windows on a menu. Create tags and attach them
    to items.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Generate images">
    Generate item or category photos from names and descriptions. Improve
    existing photos.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Landing page">
    Update the hero image or content blocks on your venue landing page.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Staff and billing">
    Owner-only: generate magic links for staff, start Stripe checkout,
    change the venue slug.
  </Card>
  <Card title="Read anything">
    Ask "what's my most expensive dish" or "which categories have no
    photos yet" — reads run instantly, no **Apply change** needed.
  </Card>
</Columns>

## How changes get applied

<Steps>
  <Step title="You describe what you want">
    In plain language: *"Add a pizza category with four items: Margherita
    €9, Pepperoni €10, Quattro Formaggi €11, Marinara €8."*
  </Step>
  <Step title="The assistant drafts the change">
    You see a **confirmation card** with the actual change laid out —
    names, prices, whatever applies. No UUIDs, no raw JSON.
  </Step>
  <Step title="You tap Apply change">
    The assistant runs the real action against your workspace. You see a
    short summary: "Done — created four items in Pizza."
  </Step>
  <Step title="Undo if you need to">
    Some creates (new items, new categories) come with an **Undo last
    change** button. Edits are reversed by telling the assistant to undo
    them, or editing in the menu builder.
  </Step>
</Steps>

<Note>
The assistant never changes anything without your **Apply change** tap.
Reads are instant; writes always wait for you.
</Note>

## Role limits

- **Owners** can use everything: menu edits, schedules, tags, images,
  landing page, staff magic links, Stripe checkout, slug changes.
- **Staff** can use most menu edits but can't run owner-only actions
  (staff management, billing, slug). The assistant refuses and tells
  you why.

## Live support

Alongside the assistant there's a separate **live chat** control for
human support. It's a different button, powered by Intercom, and
available only on workspace pages.

## Turning off the AI assistant

If you'd rather not have the assistant, your admin can disable it for
your venue. The workspace still works the same way — you just won't see
the floating button.
